name: Command Tent
prompt:
Vietnam War firebase command/GP (general purpose) tent. Standard US military canvas tent for command post or quarters.

Physical structure:
- Frame: ridge-pole tent, rectangular
  - ~4m x 3m base footprint
  - ~2.5m tall at center ridge
  - Ridge pole runs along length (X axis)
  - Sloped roof from ridge to sides
- Canvas: OD green canvas covering
  - Roof panels: two angled panels meeting at ridge
  - Side walls: ~1.5m tall vertical walls, canvas
  - Front: rolled-up flap entrance (open, showing interior)
  - Rear: closed panel
  - Guy ropes: 4-6 thin lines from roof edges to ground stakes (simplified)
- Poles: center poles (2) visible inside through open entrance
  - Wooden or metal uprights supporting ridge
- Floor: raised wooden platform or ground-level canvas floor
- Details:
  - Sandbag wall: small sandbag protection wall along one side, 2 bags high
  - Antenna: radio whip antenna near entrance

Colors: OD green canvas (0x5a6b4a), darker shadows (0x4a5a3a), wood poles (0x8B7355), sandbags (0xc2a878), rope (0x8a7a5a)
Orientation: Entrance facing +Z, at ground level
Scale: Real-world meters (~4m x 3m, ~2.5m tall)
